channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The FCC just saved Netgear from its router ban for no obvious reason
item.description: The United States' foreign router ban didn't make a whole lot of sense, and today may not change that. The FCC has just granted Netgear a conditional approval to import its future consumer...

item.title: Malware-Based Attacks: The Undying Threat of the Computer Virus
item.description: When most people hear "hacking," they picture a hooded figure pounding a keyboard. In reality, some of the most devastating breaches start with a single, silent, self-replicating line of code: the...

item.title: Why Claude Code Changed How I Freelance as a Developer
item.description: I've been freelancing for years — React dashboards, Node.js APIs, the usual. In early 2026, I started using Claude Code as my primary development tool. Three months later, my workflow is...

item.title: How I Built a Multi-Agent System Using Only Claude Code (No Frameworks)
item.description: I run 7 Claude Code agents that coordinate across 16 projects on a single machine. No CrewAI. No AutoGen. No LangGraph. Just files, shell scripts, and Claude Code's built-in hook system. This...

item.title: Tool-Chain Automation: Using Ansible to Deploy Terraform and Web Content
item.description: Automation doesn't stop at OS updates. Today, I expanded my Ansible master playbook to handle two very different, but equally important, tasks: ​Software Provisioning: Used the unarchive module to...

item.title: I Open-Sourced the Most Overkill Claude Code Setup — 15 Agents, 17 Hooks, 60-99% Token Savings
item.description: I kept burning through Claude Code's Max plan limits way too fast. So I built an entire optimization and quality enforcement system and open-sourced it. 🔗 claude-god-mode on GitHub The Problem...

item.title: Every compliance audit follows the same pattern:
item.description: Auditor: "Show me evidence this change was authorized and tested." Engineering team: spends 2-3 hours digging through GitHub, Jira, CI logs, and Slack to piece together a narrative Auditor:...

item.title: You've been writing Sumerish for years. You just didn't know it had a name.
item.description: Look at this code: const isLoading = true; const hasError = false; const canSubmit = false; You already know what those variables contain without reading the rest of the file. is → boolean. has →...

item.title: CVEs are vulnerabilities(!!!) and part 2 of my Notion automation
item.description: Read part 1 first over here. Last week I learned that there are people in tech who don't know what a CVE is. This shocked me to my core! I'm not talking about people not knowing the acronym,...

item.title: Why AI React Component Generators Break in Real Projects (And How to Fix It)
item.description: Why AI React Component Generators Break in Real Projects (And How to Fix It) Most AI React component generators look impressive… Until you try to use them in a real production codebase. That’s...

item.title: Spec Driven Development with ZeeSpec : greenfield vs brownfield
item.description: A practical guide to applying Spec-Driven Development for both new builds and legacy systems The AI Specification Problem Nobody Talks About AI coding assistants have changed how software gets...

item.title: Dozens of 4K Blu-rays are included in Amazon’s three for $33 sale
item.description: Amazon has switched on its semi-regular three for $33 sale for a large batch of 4K Blu-ray movies. As usual, there are too many to list individually, though it’s a comprehensive mix of old and new...

item.title: Prime Video is bundling Apple TV Plus and Peacock for a limited time
item.description: Amazon is offering Prime Video subscribers a way to get Apple TV Plus and Peacock Premium Plus at a discount. With a new bundle, subscribers can access all content from both services directly...

item.title: Privacy advocates want Google to stop handing consumer data over to ICE
item.description: The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) is asking the attorneys general of California and New York to investigate Google for deceptive trade practices, saying the tech giant fails to notify users...

item.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
item.description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

item.title: The attacks on Sam Altman are a warning for the AI world
item.description: Before allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, the 20-year-old accused attacker wrote about his fear that the AI race would cause humans to go extinct, the San...

item.title: Redefining the future of software engineering
item.description: Software engineering has experienced two seismic shifts this century. First was the rise of the open source movement, which gradually made code accessible to developers and engineers everywhere....

item.title: Elon Musk grilled by senator over X Money plans
item.description: Elon Musk is facing questions about his upcoming payments platform, X Money, and whether it will allow users to safely perform transactions. In a letter to Musk on Tuesday, Sen. Elizabeth Warren...

item.title: You can grab LG’s B5 OLED TV for over 50 percent off right now
item.description: ​​If you’re looking to get OLED picture quality without spending a couple thousand dollars, LG’s 48-inch B5 OLED TV is on sale for $599.99 ($700 off) at Best Buy, which is one of the best prices...

item.title: Leaked images reveal a dual-lens pro version of DJI’s next Osmo Pocket camera
item.description: Reliable leaker Igor Bogdanov shared an image on X yesterday of an unseen person holding two versions of a handheld stabilized camera, including one that's rumored to be a pro version of the DJI...

item.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
item.description: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to...

item.title: Google’s Spotlight-like desktop search bar for Windows is available for everyone
item.description: Last year, Google announced that it was testing a Google desktop app for Windows that resembles macOS's Spotlight feature, and now the app is available globally in English. You can download the...

item.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
item.description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

item.title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
item.description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

item.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
item.description: Video showing Skills in Chrome

item.title: Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’
item.description: Google is launching a new Chrome workflow feature that allows you to reuse your favorite Gemini commands across multiple webpages. Any AI prompts can now be saved as "Skills" in the Chrome desktop...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m speaking at DemocracyXChange 2026 in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on April 18, 2026. I’m speaking at the SANS AI Cybersecurity...

item.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released
item.description: Two high-severity security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in Composer, a package manager for PHP, that, if successfully exploited, could result in arbitrary command execution. The...

item.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it. There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

item.title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.
